page: Amazon Web Services r8gb.12xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Graviton processor family. It features 48 dedicated physical cores utilizing the ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 architecture running at 2.8 GHz. The instance is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, providing a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio (8.0 GB per core). It operates on the Nitro hypervisor and includes a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps. There is no local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration bundled with this instance.

Benchmark results indicate strong memory and multi-core performance. The instance achieves top-tier ratings in memory bandwidth, with read-only large block speeds of 434.99 GB/sec and write large block speeds of 216.28 GB/sec. Multi-threaded operations are highly efficient, as demonstrated by top-tier scores in Geekbench multi-core (23666.0), software compilation with Clang (71057.0), and Passmark database operations (47920.83). Single-core performance is moderate, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1706.78.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 architecture delivers high resource density and memory bandwidth per unit of compute, making it highly competitive for memory-bound workloads. However, the lack of local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, and the absence of x86-specific instruction sets represents a tradeoff for legacy applications. This instance is highly suited for memory-intensive workloads, including large-scale database operations, Redis caching layers, static web serving, and multi-threaded data processing.
description: Amazon Web Services r8gb.12xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance powered by 48 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 physical cores running at 2.8 GHz. It features 384.0 GB of RAM, yielding an 8.0 GB per core ratio, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor with 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark data highlights top-tier memory performance, with large-block read speeds of 434.99 GB/sec and write speeds of 216.28 GB/sec, alongside strong multi-core capabilities in software compilation and database operations. While single-core performance is moderate and the ARM64 architecture lacks x86 vector extensions, the high memory-to-vCPU ratio offers excellent qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound applications. The r8gb.12xlarge is optimized for database operations, high-throughput web serving, caching layers like Redis, and memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8gb.12xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance featuring 48 Graviton cores, 384 GB RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier memory and database performance.

tagline: A high-bandwidth, memory-optimized ARM64 instance designed for demanding database operations, caching layers, and memory-intensive enterprise workloads.

384.0 GB of system memory (8.0 GB per core ratio)
Top-tier memory bandwidth with 434.99 GB/sec read speeds
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via Nitro hypervisor
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for database operations, Redis caching, and web serving
